$comment: Runaway detection only. There is deliberately NO parallelism cap and NO hard budget kill. These values exist to catch a subagent that is stuck or looping, not to ration work.
$maxParallel: 0 means unlimited: launch every package whose dependencies are satisfied. pi-subagents defaults `concurrency` to 4, so skills MUST pass concurrency explicitly (= wave size) or this decision is silently overridden. Any positive integer here caps it.
$packageTimeoutMin: Passed as timeoutMs. A subagent exceeding it is killed, logged as a timeout, and retried within maxRetriesPerPackage. Timeouts are always reported in the wave summary, never swallowed.
$turnBudget: Loop guard: an agent taking this many turns on one work package is stuck, not thorough.
$toolBudget: Second loop guard, catching repeated identical tool calls that burn turns slowly.
$control: pi-subagents native stuck detection: no observed activity for 5 minutes, or 3 consecutive failing mutating tool calls, raises the run to needs_attention so the orchestrator can surface it.
$maxRetriesPerPackage: After exhaustion, escalate once to oracle, then mark the package blocked, continue with independent packages, and stop to report.
$softBudget: When cumulative reported subagent cost crosses the threshold, finish the in-flight wave, then ask: continue / raise / stop. Never abort mid-package. If pi-subagents reports no usage data, say so once and treat the soft budget as disabled rather than estimating.
